text: The surgeon performs a robotic-assisted hand-sewn anastomosis on the distal segment of the colon, which is too long for an EA stapler. The posterior layer of the anastomosis is created using a running IIO silk suture, incorporating the staple line to oversew it and form the posterior serosal layer.

The surgeon creates enterotomies on both the distal and proximal colon, spanning the width of the staple line for a wide anastomosis. The mucosal layer is closed in two directions using separate 3-0 Vicryl sutures, with the third robotic arm maintaining tension on the suture line.

The surgeon uses a vicryl suture to complete the anterior portion of the anastomosis, then begins a second vicryl suture on the back layer, proceeding in the opposite direction. The mucosal layer is completed as the two vicryl sutures meet in the middle and are tied together. The front layer is closed with a separate running silk suture, which is tied at the end, and all suture material is removed, completing the anastomosis.
The completed anastomosis is shown lying tension-free within the abdominal cavity.
